title: Re-import a repeated or overlapping statement safely
preconditions: Prior canonical writes retain source provenance and idempotency identities; the new source can be fingerprinted; the adapter defines deterministic identity within its source context, while cross-file identity may be unavailable.
postconditions: Exact replay and exact row duplication create no additional canonical transaction; uncertain overlap is explicit and uncommitted; source files and prior receipts remain unchanged.
main_flow: 1. INGEST compares the source fingerprint and adapter contract with prior batch evidence. 2. Byte-identical content under the same supported variant is treated as Exact Replay regardless of filename. 3. When the adapter provides identity-grade cross-file evidence, already committed identities with identical facts are reported as Exact Duplicates and are not written again. 4. New proven identities remain eligible candidates. 5. Similar, overlapping, or corrected records without identity-grade evidence are surfaced as blocking Overlap Conflicts. 6. The owner receives a preview accounting for prior, new, and conflicted outcomes before any commit.
alt_flows: 1a. Prior provenance is unavailable or incompatible -> do not claim automatic deduplication
2a. Content is changed despite a familiar filename -> treat it as a new preview, not exact replay
3a. A reused identity carries different facts -> return stable conflict and preserve the original transaction
5a. Same-amount/date/description records lack exact identity -> never silently collapse them as duplicates
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner previews a statement whose content or date window may overlap an earlier import.","status":"draft"}
